title: Reimburse Ikigai Info Governance Action Deposit.

rationale.summary: Vote: NEEDS_MORE_INFO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), critical information needed for a responsible directional vote is still missing. Decisive reason: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "An Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork." though 3 review blocker(s) remain open. What would enable a directional vote: Provide: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting; Provide: Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections. Residual uncertainty: Dossier completeness not confirmed for this treasury proposal. Confidence context: score 0.00; confidence 20.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Directional voting is blocked until dossier quality gates pass.
top_fixes: Provide: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting.
Provide: Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections.
Provide: Missing dossier section: risk analysis
missing_evidence: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting.
Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to reimburse the lost deposit for the Ikigai Info governance action, plus compensation for staking rewards.
Recipient: the Ikigai Info governance action submitter
Deliverables: Instant on-chain distribution of funds to the intended recipient upon enactment
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): An Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork.
Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, low materiality): The Ikigai Info governance action was symbolic and asked whether the community agreed with a statement of thanks and hope for Cardano's future.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): A Cardano node bug allowed an unregistered stake key to be used for the governance action, preventing the submitter from recovering the 100,000 ADA deposit.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 103,000 ADA, consisting of the original 100,000 ADA deposit plus 3,000 ADA for lost staking rewards calculated at 2% per year.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal states there were significant community sentiments at the time that the Ikigai Info governance action deposit should be reimbursed.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The Cardano in Oceania initiative included this reimbursement in a budget Info governance action, but that governance action was not approved.
missing: Independent evidence for: A Cardano node bug allowed an unregistered stake key to be used for the governance action, preventing the submitter from recovering the 100,000 ADA deposit.
Independent evidence for: The proposal states there were significant community sentiments at the time that the Ikigai Info governance action deposit should be reimbursed.
Independent evidence for: The Cardano in Oceania initiative included this reimbursement in a budget Info governance action, but that governance action was not approved.
Independent evidence for: The recipient has not previously received funds from the treasury.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "An Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork." though 3 review blocker(s) remain open.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "A Cardano node bug allowed an unregistered stake key to be used for the governance action, preventing the submitter from recovering the 100,000 ADA deposit." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: No links to forum discussion or community feedback
Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
